creator: mikael@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to David Chisnall from comment #3)
It works fine on real hw.

I'm seeing this syscall trace on qemu-arm-static:
open("-L",0,0666) = -1 errno=2 (No such file or directory)
write(2,0x454c7000,55)The source property list must contain an NSDictionary.

I put some debugging printf in pl2link (lang/gnustep-base):
on qemu-arm-static I see:
[args count] = 4
[args objectAtIndex: 0] = 'qemu-arm-static'
[args objectAtIndex: 1] = '-L'
[args objectAtIndex: 2] = '/usr/gnemul/qemu-arm'
[args objectAtIndex: 3] = './pl2link'

while on real hw I see:
[args count] = 1
[args objectAtIndex: 0] = './pl2link'

gnustep-gui builds fine with qemu if gnustep-base is compiled with --enable-pass-arguments:
http://mikael.urankar.free.fr/FreeBSD/arm/build_logs/gnustep-gui-0.24.1.log


On a side note, /nxb-bin is hardcoded in config.make and causes build problem when packages are used and the host doesn't have nxb-bin
grep nxb-bin /usr/local/GNUstep/System/Library/Makefiles/config.make
CC  = /nxb-bin/usr/bin/cc
CC  = /nxb-bin/usr/bin/cc
CXX = /nxb-bin/usr/bin/c++
CXX = /nxb-bin/usr/bin/c++
CPP = /nxb-bin/usr/bin/cpp
